key_findings.finding: TimeFilter WIP in home.tsx — already has time filter UI and logic, but hardcoded English labels
No test file exists for home.tsx — the session search/filter pipeline is untested
Current text search uses naive String.includes() — fuzzysort is already available as a dependency
useFilteredList hook from @opencode-ai/ui/hooks is available but uses async createResource — not ideal for local in-memory session filtering where synchronous is fine
settings.tsx has showSessionViz and onboarded boolean settings recently added by another lane — may need to add filter persistence settings
Session type in SDK has agent? and model? fields — filter-by-agent and filter-by-model are feasible at the data model level

smoking_guns.title: TimeFilter labels are hardcoded English — will break in non-English locales immediately
smoking_guns.detail: TIME_FILTER_LABELS at home.tsx:57-62 uses literal strings ('All Sessions', 'Today', etc.) instead of language.t() calls. Every other i18n key in the component uses language.t() (home.sessions.search.placeholder, home.sessions.group.today, etc.). This is a localization regression that will ship if the dirty changes are committed as-is.

smoking_guns.title: No search input debounce — every keystroke triggers full re-filter of all sessions
smoking_guns.detail: The onInput handler directly calls setState('search', value) which triggers reactive re-evaluation of the records memo (which iterates all sessions). For projects with many sessions, this will cause jank. @solid-primitives/scheduled debounce is already available as a dependency.

smoking_guns.title: Text search only covers title+projectName — misses session summary and message content
smoking_guns.detail: The current implementation `${session.title} ${projectName}`.toLowerCase().includes(value) ignores session.summary (which often contains the actual task description). Users searching for a task description won't find it unless it matches the auto-generated title.
